common.h(3) Library Functions Manual common.h(3)

NAME

common.h - Common declarations and data structures for cryptographic handshake.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct crypto_handshake_context_t
Cryptographic handshake context structure.

Macros

Authentication Requirement Flags
Flags sent in AUTH_CHALLENGE packet to indicate server requirements.



#define AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD 0x01
Server requires password authentication. #define AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY 0x02
Server requires client key authentication (whitelist).

Typedefs


typedef struct crypto_handshake_context_t crypto_handshake_context_t
Cryptographic handshake context structure.

Enumerations


enum crypto_handshake_state_t { CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DISABLED = 0, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_FAILED }
Cryptographic handshake state enumeration.

Functions

Common Handshake Functions



asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_init (const char *name, crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, bool is_server)
Initialize named crypto handshake context. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_set_parameters (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, const crypto_parameters_packet_t *params)
Set crypto parameters from crypto_parameters_packet_t. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, uint16_t packet_type, size_t packet_size)
Validate crypto packet size based on session parameters. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_init_with_password (const char *name, crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, bool is_server, const char *password)
Initialize named crypto handshake context with password authentication. void crypto_handshake_destroy (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx)
Cleanup crypto handshake context with secure memory wiping. bool crypto_handshake_is_ready (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx)
Check if handshake is complete and encryption is ready. const crypto_context_t * crypto_handshake_get_context (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx)
Get the crypto context for encryption/decryption. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_encrypt_packet (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
Encrypt a packet using the established crypto context. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_decrypt_packet (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
Decrypt a packet using the established crypto context. asciichat_error_t crypto_encrypt_packet_or_passthrough (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, bool crypto_ready, const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
Encrypt with automatic passthrough if crypto not ready. asciichat_error_t crypto_decrypt_packet_or_passthrough (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, bool crypto_ready, const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
Decrypt with automatic passthrough if crypto not ready.

Session Rekeying Functions



asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_rekey_request (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t socket)
Send REKEY_REQUEST packet (initiator side). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_rekey_response (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t socket)
Send REKEY_RESPONSE packet (responder side). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_rekey_complete (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t socket)
Send REKEY_COMPLETE packet (initiator side). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_process_rekey_request (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet (responder side). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_process_rekey_response (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_RESPONSE packet (initiator side). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_process_rekey_complete (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_COMPLETE packet (responder side). bool crypto_handshake_should_rekey (const crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx)
Check if rekeying should be triggered for this handshake context.

Detailed Description

Common declarations and data structures for cryptographic handshake.

Definition in file crypto/handshake/common.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY 0x02

Server requires client key authentication (whitelist).

Definition at line 26 of file crypto/handshake/common.h.

Referenced by crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(), and crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge().

#define AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD 0x01

Server requires password authentication.

Definition at line 24 of file crypto/handshake/common.h.

Referenced by crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(), and crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge().

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum crypto_handshake_state_t

Cryptographic handshake state enumeration. Represents the current state of the handshake protocol. Handshake follows a strict state machine - invalid transitions return errors.

Enumerator

CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DISABLED
No encryption (handshake disabled)
CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT
Initial state (ready to start handshake)
CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE
DH key exchange in progress
CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING
Authentication challenge/response
CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY
Handshake complete, encryption ready
CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_FAILED
Handshake failed (cannot recover)

Definition at line 38 of file crypto/handshake/common.h.

38              {
39   CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DISABLED = 0,   
40   CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT,           
41   CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE,   
42   CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING, 
43   CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,          
44   CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_FAILED          
45 } crypto_handshake_state_t;










Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t crypto_decrypt_packet_or_passthrough
  (const crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, bool crypto_ready,
  const uint8_t * ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *
  plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t * plaintext_len)


Decrypt with automatic passthrough if crypto not ready.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context


crypto_ready True if crypto is ready, false to passthrough


ciphertext Ciphertext or plaintext data to decrypt


ciphertext_len Length of ciphertext/plaintext data


plaintext Output buffer for plaintext


plaintext_size Size of output buffer


plaintext_len Output parameter for actual plaintext length


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 363 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


366                                                                               {
367   if (!crypto_ready) {
368     // No encryption - just copy data
369     if (ciphertext_len > plaintext_size) {
370       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER, "Ciphertext too large for plaintext buffer: %zu > %zu", ciphertext_len, plaintext_size);
371       return ERROR_BUFFER;
372     }
373     memcpy(plaintext, ciphertext, ciphertext_len);
374     *plaintext_len = ciphertext_len;
375     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
376   }
377 
378   return crypto_handshake_decrypt_packet(ctx, ciphertext, ciphertext_len, plaintext, plaintext_size, plaintext_len);
379 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_decrypt_packet(), ERROR_BUFFER, and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by crypto_client_decrypt_packet(), and
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet().






asciichat_error_t crypto_encrypt_packet_or_passthrough
  (const crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, bool crypto_ready,
  const uint8_t * plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *
  ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t * ciphertext_len)


Encrypt with automatic passthrough if crypto not ready.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context


crypto_ready True if crypto is ready, false to passthrough


plaintext Plaintext data to encrypt


plaintext_len Length of plaintext data


ciphertext Output buffer for ciphertext or plaintext (if passthrough)


ciphertext_size Size of output buffer


ciphertext_len Output parameter for actual length


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

    crypto/handshake/common.c.


347                                                                                {
348   if (!crypto_ready) {
349     // No encryption - just copy data

352       return ERROR_BUFFER;
353     }
354     memcpy(ciphertext, plaintext, plaintext_len);
355     *ciphertext_len = plaintext_len;

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_decrypt_packet (const
  crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, const uint8_t * ciphertext,
  size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t * plaintext, size_t plaintext_size,
  size_t * plaintext_len)


Decrypt a packet using the established crypto context.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be ready)


ciphertext Ciphertext data to decrypt


ciphertext_len Length of ciphertext data


plaintext Output buffer for plaintext


plaintext_size Size of output buffer


plaintext_len Output parameter for actual plaintext length


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 326 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


328                                                                          {
329   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
330     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx, ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
331     return ERROR_INVALID_STATE;
332   }
333 
334   crypto_result_t result = crypto_decrypt((crypto_context_t *)&ctx->crypto_ctx, ciphertext, ciphertext_len, plaintext,
335                                           plaintext_size, plaintext_len);
336   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
337     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to decrypt packet: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
338   }
339 
340   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
341 }

    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, crypto_decrypt(),
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_NETWORK, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by crypto_decrypt_packet_or_passthrough().






void
  crypto_handshake_destroy (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx)


Cleanup crypto handshake context with secure memory wiping.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context to cleanup


Definition at line 282 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


282                                                                {
283   if (!ctx)
284     return;
285 
286   // Cleanup core crypto context
287   crypto_destroy(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
288 
289   // Zero out sensitive data
290   sodium_memzero(ctx, sizeof(crypto_handshake_context_t));
291 }

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_encrypt_packet (const
  crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, const uint8_t * plaintext,
  size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t * ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size,
  size_t * ciphertext_len)


Encrypt a packet using the established crypto context.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be ready)


plaintext Plaintext data to encrypt


plaintext_len Length of plaintext data


ciphertext Output buffer for ciphertext


ciphertext_size Size of output buffer


ciphertext_len Output parameter for actual ciphertext length


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 308 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


310                                                                           {
311   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
312     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx, ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
313     return ERROR_INVALID_STATE;
314   }
315 
316   crypto_result_t result = crypto_encrypt((crypto_context_t *)&ctx->crypto_ctx, plaintext, plaintext_len, ciphertext,
317                                           ciphertext_size, ciphertext_len);
318   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
319     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to encrypt packet: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
320   }
321 
322   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
323 }

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_init (const char *
  name, crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, bool is_server)


Initialize named crypto handshake context.


Parameters


name Debug name for the crypto context (typically
  "crypto_client_<id>")


ctx Handshake context to initialize (must not be NULL)


is_server True if this is the server side, false for client


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


The context is automatically registered with the debug naming
    system.


Definition at line 18 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


18                                                                                                            {
19   if (!name) {
20     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Crypto context name is required");
21   }
22 
23   if (!ctx) {
24     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p", ctx);
25   }
26 
27   // Register crypto context with debug naming system
28   NAMED_REGISTER_CRYPTO_CONTEXT(ctx, name, NULL);
29 
30   // Zero out the context
31   memset(ctx, 0, sizeof(crypto_handshake_context_t));
32 
33   // Initialize core crypto context
34 #ifndef NDEBUG
35   log_info("★★★ BEFORE crypto_init()");
36   debug_sync_print_state();
37 #endif
38   crypto_result_t result = crypto_init(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
39 #ifndef NDEBUG
40   log_info("★★★ AFTER crypto_init(), result=%d", result);
41   debug_sync_print_state();
42 #endif
43   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
44     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to initialize crypto context: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
45   }
46 
47   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT;
48   ctx->is_server = is_server;
49   ctx->verify_server_key = false;
50   ctx->require_client_auth = false;
51   ctx->server_uses_client_auth = false; // Set to true only if authenticated packet received
52 
53   // Load server keys if this is a server
54   if (is_server) {
55     log_dev("Server crypto handshake initialized (ephemeral keys)");
56   } else {
57     log_dev("Client crypto handshake initialized");
58   }
59 
60   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
61 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT,
    crypto_init(), CRYPTO_OK, crypto_result_to_string(),
    debug_sync_print_state(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, crypto_handshake_context_t::is_server,
    log_dev, log_info, NAMED_REGISTER_CRYPTO_CONTEXT,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_uses_client_auth,
    SET_ERRNO, crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and
    crypto_handshake_context_t::verify_server_key.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(),
    client_crypto_init(), client_generate_keypair(),
    client_handle_key_exchange_init(), and
    server_crypto_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_init_with_password
  (const char * name, crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, bool
  is_server, const char * password)


Initialize named crypto handshake context with password
    authentication.


Parameters


name Debug name for the crypto context (typically
  "crypto_client_<id>")


ctx Handshake context to initialize (must not be NULL)


is_server True if this is the server side, false for client


password Password for authentication (must meet length
  requirements)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


The context is automatically registered with the debug naming
    system.


Definition at line 245 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


246                                                                             {
247   if (!name) {
248     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Crypto context name is required");
249   }
250 
251   if (!ctx || !password) {
252     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, password=%p", ctx, password);
253   }
254 
255   // Register crypto context with debug naming system
256   NAMED_REGISTER_CRYPTO_CONTEXT(ctx, name, NULL);
257 
258   // Zero out the context
259   memset(ctx, 0, sizeof(crypto_handshake_context_t));
260 
261   // Initialize core crypto context with password
262   crypto_result_t result = crypto_init_with_password(&ctx->crypto_ctx, password);
263   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
264     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to initialize crypto context with password: %s",
265                      crypto_result_to_string(result));
266   }
267 
268   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT;
269   ctx->is_server = is_server;
270   ctx->verify_server_key = false;
271   ctx->require_client_auth = false;
272   ctx->server_uses_client_auth = false; // Set to true only if authenticated packet received
273   ctx->has_password = true;
274 
275   // Store password temporarily (will be cleared after key derivation)
276   SAFE_STRNCPY(ctx->password, password, sizeof(ctx->password) - 1);
277 
278   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
279 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT,
    crypto_init_with_password(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, crypto_handshake_context_t::has_password,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::is_server,
    NAMED_REGISTER_CRYPTO_CONTEXT,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::password,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_uses_client_auth,
    SET_ERRNO, crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and
    crypto_handshake_context_t::verify_server_key.


Referenced by client_crypto_init(), and
    server_crypto_handshake().






bool crypto_handshake_is_ready (const
  crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx)


Check if handshake is complete and encryption is ready.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context


Returns


true if handshake is complete and encryption is ready,
  false otherwise


Definition at line 294 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


294                                                                       {
295   if (!ctx)
296     return false;
297   return ctx->state == CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY && crypto_is_ready(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
298 }






References crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY, crypto_is_ready(), and
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state.

    client_send_thread_func(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    crypto_handshake_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_handshake_encrypt_packet(),

    crypto_handshake_process_rekey_request(),

    crypto_handshake_rekey_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_rekey_request(),
    crypto_handshake_rekey_response(),

  crypto_server_is_ready().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_process_rekey_complete
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, const uint8_t * packet,
  size_t packet_len)


Process received REKEY_COMPLETE packet (responder side).


Parameters


ctx Crypto handshake context (must be ready)


packet Encrypted packet (empty payload, encrypted with NEW key)


packet_len Packet length


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Process received REKEY_COMPLETE packet (responder side). Verifies
    that the packet decrypts with the new shared secret. If successful, commits
    to the new key.


Definition at line 587 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


588                                                                              {
589   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
590     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Handshake not ready for rekeying: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx,
591                      ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
592   }
593 
594   if (!ctx->crypto_ctx.rekey_in_progress || !ctx->crypto_ctx.has_temp_key) {
595     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "No rekey in progress or temp key missing");
596   }
597 
598   log_debug("Received REKEY_COMPLETE packet (%zu bytes), verifying with NEW key", packet_len);
599 
600   // Temporarily swap keys to decrypt with NEW key
601   uint8_t old_shared_key[CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE];
602   memcpy(old_shared_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key, CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE);
603   memcpy(ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.temp_shared_key, CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE);
604 
605   // Attempt to decrypt with NEW key
606   uint8_t plaintext[256];
607   size_t plaintext_len = 0;
608   crypto_result_t result =
609       crypto_decrypt(&ctx->crypto_ctx, packet, packet_len, plaintext, sizeof(plaintext), &plaintext_len);
610 
611   // Restore old key immediately
612   memcpy(ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key, old_shared_key, CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE);
613   sodium_memzero(old_shared_key, sizeof(old_shared_key));
614 
615   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
616     crypto_rekey_abort(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
617     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "REKEY_COMPLETE decryption failed (key mismatch): %s",
618                      crypto_result_to_string(result));
619   }
620 
621   log_debug("REKEY_COMPLETE verified successfully, committing to new key");
622 
623   // Commit to new key (atomic switch)
624   result = crypto_rekey_commit(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
625   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
626     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to commit rekey: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
627   }
628 
629   log_debug("Session rekeying completed successfully (responder side)");
630   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
631 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, crypto_decrypt(),
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_rekey_abort(), crypto_rekey_commit(),
    crypto_result_to_string(), CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    crypto_context_t::has_temp_key, log_debug,
    crypto_context_t::rekey_in_progress, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_context_t::shared_key, and
    crypto_context_t::temp_shared_key.






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_process_rekey_request
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, const uint8_t * packet,
  size_t packet_len)


Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet (responder side).


Parameters


ctx Crypto handshake context (must be ready)


packet Packet payload (32-byte public key)


packet_len Packet length (should be 32)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet (responder side). Extracts
    peer's new ephemeral public key and computes new shared secret.


Definition at line 501 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


502                                                                             {
503   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
504     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Handshake not ready for rekeying: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx,
505                      ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
506   }
507 
508   // DDoS PROTECTION: Rate limit rekey requests
509   time_t now = time(NULL);
510   if (ctx->crypto_ctx.rekey_last_request_time > 0) {
511     time_t elapsed = now - ctx->crypto_ctx.rekey_last_request_time;
512     time_t min_request_interval_seconds = (time_t)(REKEY_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
513     if (elapsed < min_request_interval_seconds) {
514       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO,
515                        "SECURITY: Rekey request rejected - too frequent (%ld sec since last, minimum %ld sec required)",
516                        (long)elapsed, (long)min_request_interval_seconds);
517     }
518   }
519 
520   // Update last request time
521   ctx->crypto_ctx.rekey_last_request_time = now;
522 
523   // Validate packet size (should be 32 bytes for X25519 public key)
524   if (packet_len != CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) {
525     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid REKEY_REQUEST packet size: %zu (expected %d)", packet_len,
526                      CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
527   }
528 
529   log_debug("Received REKEY_REQUEST with peer's new ephemeral public key (32 bytes)");
530 
531   // Initialize our rekey process (generates our new ephemeral keypair)
532   crypto_result_t result = crypto_rekey_init(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
533   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
534     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to initialize rekey: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
535   }
536 
537   // Process peer's public key and compute new shared secret
538   result = crypto_rekey_process_request(&ctx->crypto_ctx, packet);
539   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
540     crypto_rekey_abort(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
541     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process REKEY_REQUEST: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
542   }
543 
544   log_debug("REKEY_REQUEST processed successfully, new shared secret computed (responder side)");
545   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
546 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), CRYPTO_OK,
    CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, crypto_rekey_abort(),
    crypto_rekey_init(), crypto_rekey_process_request(),
    crypto_result_to_string(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, log_debug,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, crypto_context_t::rekey_last_request_time,
    REKEY_MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by crypto_client_process_rekey_request().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_process_rekey_response
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, const uint8_t * packet,
  size_t packet_len)


Process received REKEY_RESPONSE packet (initiator side).


Parameters


ctx Crypto handshake context (must be ready)


packet Packet payload (32-byte public key)


packet_len Packet length (should be 32)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Process received REKEY_RESPONSE packet (initiator side). Extracts
    peer's new ephemeral public key and computes new shared secret.


Definition at line 552 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


553                                                                              {
554   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
555     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Handshake not ready for rekeying: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx,
556                      ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
557   }
558 
559   // Validate packet size (should be 32 bytes for X25519 public key)
560   if (packet_len != CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) {
561     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid REKEY_RESPONSE packet size: %zu (expected %d)", packet_len,
562                      CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
563   }
564 
565   if (!ctx->crypto_ctx.rekey_in_progress || !ctx->crypto_ctx.has_temp_key) {
566     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "No rekey in progress or temp key missing");
567   }
568 
569   log_debug("Received REKEY_RESPONSE with peer's new ephemeral public key (32 bytes)");
570 
571   // Process peer's public key and compute new shared secret
572   crypto_result_t result = crypto_rekey_process_response(&ctx->crypto_ctx, packet);
573   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
574     crypto_rekey_abort(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
575     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process REKEY_RESPONSE: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
576   }
577 
578   log_debug("REKEY_RESPONSE processed successfully, new shared secret computed (initiator side)");
579   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
580 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), CRYPTO_OK,
    CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, crypto_rekey_abort(),
    crypto_rekey_process_response(), crypto_result_to_string(),
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    crypto_context_t::has_temp_key, log_debug,
    crypto_context_t::rekey_in_progress, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by crypto_client_process_rekey_response().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_rekey_complete
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t socket)


Send REKEY_COMPLETE packet (initiator side).


Parameters


ctx Crypto handshake context (must be ready)


socket Socket to send on


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Send REKEY_COMPLETE packet (initiator side). Note: This packet is
    encrypted with the new shared secret. It proves that both sides have
    computed the same shared secret.


Definition at line 446 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


446                                                                                                     {
447   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
448     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Handshake not ready for rekeying: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx,
449                      ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
450   }
451 
452   if (!ctx->crypto_ctx.rekey_in_progress || !ctx->crypto_ctx.has_temp_key) {
453     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "No rekey in progress or temp key missing");
454   }
455 
456   // Encrypt empty payload with NEW key to prove possession
457   uint8_t plaintext[1] = {0}; // Minimal payload
458   uint8_t ciphertext[256];    // Sufficient for nonce + MAC + minimal payload
459   size_t ciphertext_len = 0;
460 
461   // Temporarily swap keys to encrypt with NEW key
462   uint8_t old_shared_key[CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE];
463   memcpy(old_shared_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key, CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE);
464   memcpy(ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.temp_shared_key, CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE);
465 
466   // Encrypt with NEW key
467   crypto_result_t result =
468       crypto_encrypt(&ctx->crypto_ctx, plaintext, sizeof(plaintext), ciphertext, sizeof(ciphertext), &ciphertext_len);
469 
470   // Restore old key immediately (commit will happen after successful send)
471   memcpy(ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key, old_shared_key, CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE);
472   sodium_memzero(old_shared_key, sizeof(old_shared_key));
473 
474   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
475     crypto_rekey_abort(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
476     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to encrypt REKEY_COMPLETE: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
477   }
478 
479   // Send encrypted REKEY_COMPLETE
480   log_debug("Sending REKEY_COMPLETE (encrypted with NEW key, %zu bytes)", ciphertext_len);
481   int send_result = send_packet(socket, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_REKEY_COMPLETE, ciphertext, ciphertext_len);
482   if (send_result != 0) {
483     crypto_rekey_abort(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
484     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send REKEY_COMPLETE packet");
485   }
486 
487   // Commit to new key (atomic switch)
488   result = crypto_rekey_commit(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
489   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
490     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to commit rekey: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
491   }
492 
493   log_debug("Session rekeying completed successfully (initiator side)");
494   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
495 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, crypto_encrypt(),
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_rekey_abort(), crypto_rekey_commit(),
    crypto_result_to_string(), CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_NETWORK,
    crypto_context_t::has_temp_key, log_debug,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_REKEY_COMPLETE,
    crypto_context_t::rekey_in_progress, send_packet(),
    SET_ERRNO, crypto_context_t::shared_key, and
    crypto_context_t::temp_shared_key.


Referenced by crypto_client_send_rekey_complete().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_rekey_request
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t socket)


Send REKEY_REQUEST packet (initiator side).


Parameters


ctx Crypto handshake context (must be ready)


socket Socket to send on


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Send REKEY_REQUEST packet (initiator side). Sends the initiator's
    new ephemeral public key to the peer.


Definition at line 389 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


389                                                                                                    {
390   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
391     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Handshake not ready for rekeying: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx,
392                      ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
393   }
394 
395   // Initialize rekey process (generates new ephemeral keypair)
396   crypto_result_t result = crypto_rekey_init(&ctx->crypto_ctx);
397   if (result != CRYPTO_OK) {
398     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to initialize rekey: %s", crypto_result_to_string(result));
399   }
400 
401   // Send REKEY_REQUEST with new ephemeral public key (32 bytes)
402   log_debug("Sending REKEY_REQUEST with new ephemeral X25519 public key (32 bytes)");
403   int send_result =
404       send_packet(socket, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_REKEY_REQUEST, ctx->crypto_ctx.temp_public_key, CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
405   if (send_result != 0) {
406     crypto_rekey_abort(&ctx->crypto_ctx); // Clean up temp keys on failure
407     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST packet");
408   }
409 
410   log_debug("REKEY_REQUEST sent successfully, awaiting REKEY_RESPONSE");
411   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
412 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), CRYPTO_OK,
    CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, crypto_rekey_abort(),
    crypto_rekey_init(), crypto_result_to_string(),
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_NETWORK,
    log_debug, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_REKEY_REQUEST,
    send_packet(), SET_ERRNO, and
    crypto_context_t::temp_public_key.

    crypto_client_initiate_rekey().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_rekey_response
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t socket)


Send REKEY_RESPONSE packet (responder side).


Parameters


ctx Crypto handshake context (must be ready)


socket Socket to send on


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Send REKEY_RESPONSE packet (responder side). Sends the responder's
    new ephemeral public key to the peer.


Definition at line 418 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


418                                                                                                     {
419   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
420     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Handshake not ready for rekeying: ctx=%p, ready=%d", ctx,
421                      ctx ? crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx) : 0);
422   }
423 
424   if (!ctx->crypto_ctx.rekey_in_progress || !ctx->crypto_ctx.has_temp_key) {
425     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "No rekey in progress or temp key missing");
426   }
427 
428   // Send REKEY_RESPONSE with new ephemeral public key (32 bytes)
429   log_debug("Sending REKEY_RESPONSE with new ephemeral X25519 public key (32 bytes)");
430   int send_result =
431       send_packet(socket, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_REKEY_RESPONSE, ctx->crypto_ctx.temp_public_key, CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
432   if (send_result != 0) {
433     crypto_rekey_abort(&ctx->crypto_ctx); // Clean up temp keys on failure
434     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send REKEY_RESPONSE packet");
435   }
436 
437   log_debug("REKEY_RESPONSE sent successfully, awaiting REKEY_COMPLETE");
438   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
439 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_set_parameters
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, const
  crypto_parameters_packet_t * params)


Set crypto parameters from crypto_parameters_packet_t.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context


params Negotiated crypto parameters (from capabilities negotiation)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 64 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


65                                                                                             {
66   if (!ctx || !params) {
67     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, params=%p", ctx, params);
68   }
69 
70   // Client receives network byte order and must convert
71   // Server uses host byte order and must NOT convert
72   if (ctx->is_server) {
73     // Server: values are already in host byte order
74     // Update crypto context with negotiated parameters directly
75     ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size = params->kex_public_key_size;
76     ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size = params->auth_public_key_size;
77     ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key_size = params->shared_secret_size;
78     ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size = params->signature_size;
79   } else {
80     // Client: convert from network byte order
81     // Update crypto context with negotiated parameters directly
82     ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(params->kex_public_key_size);
83     ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(params->auth_public_key_size);
84     ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(params->shared_secret_size);
85     ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(params->signature_size);
86   }
87   // Update crypto context with negotiated parameters directly
88   ctx->crypto_ctx.nonce_size = params->nonce_size;
89   ctx->crypto_ctx.mac_size = params->mac_size;
90   ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size = params->hmac_size;
91   ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size =
92       AUTH_CHALLENGE_SIZE; // Auth challenge size is fixed for now, could be negotiated later
93   ctx->crypto_ctx.encryption_key_size =
94       (uint8_t)ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key_size;                       // Use shared key size as encryption key size
95   ctx->crypto_ctx.private_key_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size; // Same as public key for X25519
96   ctx->crypto_ctx.salt_size = ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE;                     // Salt size doesn't change
97 
98   log_debug("Crypto parameters set: kex_key=%u, auth_key=%u, sig=%u, "
99             "secret=%u, nonce=%u, mac=%u, hmac=%u",
100             ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size,
101             ctx->crypto_ctx.shared_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.nonce_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.mac_size,
102             ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size);
103 
104   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
105 }






References ARGON2ID_SALT_SIZE, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    AUTH_CHALLENGE_SIZE, crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    crypto_context_t::auth_public_key_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_context_t::encryption_key_size, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    crypto_context_t::hmac_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::hmac_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::is_server,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size, log_debug,
    crypto_context_t::mac_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::mac_size, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    crypto_context_t::nonce_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::nonce_size,
    crypto_context_t::private_key_size,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size,
    crypto_context_t::salt_size, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_context_t::shared_key_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_context_t::signature_size, and
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size.

    server_crypto_handshake().

  crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx)


Check if rekeying should be triggered for this handshake
  context.


Parameters


ctx Crypto handshake context


Returns


true if rekey should be initiated, false otherwise


Check if rekeying should be triggered for this handshake context.
    Wrapper around crypto_should_rekey() for handshake context.


Definition at line 637 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


637                                                                           {
638   if (!ctx || !crypto_handshake_is_ready(ctx)) {
639     return false;
640   }
641   return crypto_should_rekey(&ctx->crypto_ctx);

References crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), and crypto_should_rekey().

    crypto_client_should_rekey().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size
  (const crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, uint16_t packet_type,
  size_t packet_size)


Validate crypto packet size based on session parameters.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must have parameters set)


packet_type Packet type to validate


packet_size Actual packet size received


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if valid, error code on failure


Definition at line 108 of file
    crypto/handshake/common.c.


109                                                                             {
110   if (!ctx) {
111     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p", ctx);
112   }
113 
114   switch (packet_type) {
115   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES:
116     if (packet_size != sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t)) {
117       // Don't  return an error code, just set the errno and return the error code
118       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid crypto capabilities packet size: %zu (expected %zu)",
119                        packet_size, sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t));
120     }
121     break;
122 
123   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS:
124     if (packet_size != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
125       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid crypto parameters packet size: %zu (expected %zu)", packet_size,
126                        sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
127     }
128     break;
129 
130   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT:
131     // Server can send either:
132     // 1. Simple format: kex_public_key_size (when server has no identity key)
133     // 2. Authenticated format: kex_public_key_size + auth_public_key_size + signature_size
134     {
135       size_t simple_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size;
136       size_t authenticated_size =
137           ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size;
138 
139       if (packet_size != simple_size && packet_size != authenticated_size) {
140         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
141                          "Invalid KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT size: %zu (expected %zu for simple or %zu for authenticated: "
142                          "kex=%u + auth=%u + sig=%u)",
143                          packet_size, simple_size, authenticated_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
144                          ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size);
145       }
146     }
147     break;
148 
149   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP:
150     // Client can send either:
151     // 1. Simple format: kex_public_key_size (when server has no identity key)
152     // 2. Authenticated format: kex_public_key_size + client_auth_key_size + client_sig_size + [gpg_key_id_len:1] +
153     // [gpg_key_id:0-16]
154     {
155       size_t simple_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size;
156       // For authenticated format, use Ed25519 sizes since client has Ed25519 key
157       size_t ed25519_auth_size = ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // Ed25519 public key is always 32 bytes
158       size_t ed25519_sig_size = ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;   // Ed25519 signature is always 64 bytes
159       size_t authenticated_min_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ed25519_auth_size + ed25519_sig_size;
160       size_t authenticated_max_size = authenticated_min_size + 1 + 40; // +1 for length, +40 for max GPG key ID
161 
162       if (packet_size != simple_size &&
163           (packet_size < authenticated_min_size || packet_size > authenticated_max_size)) {
164         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
165                          "Invalid KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP size: %zu (expected %zu for simple or %zu-%zu for authenticated: "
166                          "kex=%u + auth=%u + sig=%u + optional GPG key ID)",
167                          packet_size, simple_size, authenticated_min_size, authenticated_max_size,
168                          ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ed25519_auth_size, ed25519_sig_size);
169       }
170     }
171     break;
172 
173   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE:
174     // Server sends: 1 byte auth_flags + auth_challenge_size byte nonce
175     {
176       size_t expected_size = AUTH_CHALLENGE_FLAGS_SIZE + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
177       if (packet_size != expected_size) {
178         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid AUTH_CHALLENGE size: %zu (expected %zu: flags=%d + nonce=%u)",
179                          packet_size, expected_size, AUTH_CHALLENGE_FLAGS_SIZE, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
180       }
181     }
182     break;
183 
184   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE:
185     // Client sends: hmac_size + auth_challenge_size bytes client_nonce + [gpg_key_id_len:1] + [gpg_key_id:0-40]
186     {
187       size_t min_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
188       size_t max_size = min_size + 1 + 40; // +1 for length, +40 for max GPG key ID
189       if (packet_size < min_size || packet_size > max_size) {
190         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
191                          "Invalid AUTH_RESPONSE size: %zu (expected %zu-%zu: hmac=%u + "
192                          "nonce=%u + optional GPG key ID)",
193                          packet_size, min_size, max_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size,
194                          ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
195       }
196     }
197     break;
198 
199   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED:
200     // Variable size - just check reasonable limits
201     if (packet_size > MAX_AUTH_FAILED_PACKET_SIZE) {
202       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid AUTH_FAILED size: %zu (max %d)", packet_size,
203                        MAX_AUTH_FAILED_PACKET_SIZE);
204     }
205     break;
206 
207   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP:
208     // Server sends: hmac_size bytes
209     if (packet_size != ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size) {
210       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid SERVER_AUTH_RESP size: %zu (expected %u)", packet_size,
211                        ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size);
212     }
213     break;
214 
215   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE:
216     // Empty packet
217     if (packet_size != 0) {
218       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE size: %zu (expected 0)", packet_size);
219     }
220     break;
221 
222   case PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION:
223     // Empty packet
224     if (packet_size != 0) {
225       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid NO_ENCRYPTION size: %zu (expected 0)", packet_size);
226     }
227     break;
228 
229   case PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED:
230     // Variable size - check reasonable limits
231     if (packet_size > MAX_ENCRYPTED_PACKET_SIZE) { // 64KB max for encrypted packets
232       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid ENCRYPTED size: %zu (max %d)", packet_size,
233                        MAX_ENCRYPTED_PACKET_SIZE);
234     }
235     break;
236 
237   default:
238     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Unknown crypto packet type: %u", packet_type);
239   }
240 
241   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
242 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, AUTH_CHALLENGE_FLAGS_SIZE,
    crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    crypto_context_t::auth_public_key_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    crypto_context_t::hmac_size, MAX_AUTH_FAILED_PACKET_SIZE,
    MAX_ENCRYPTED_PACKET_SIZE, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP, PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size, SET_ERRNO, and
    crypto_context_t::signature_size.


Referenced by crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(),
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(),
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(), and
    crypto_handshake_server_complete().
